Prospect theory. This explains how people choose between different options and how they evaluate the perceived possibility of set options. Within this theory, the most famous bias is loss aversion. Basically people will behave so that they can minimize their losses. Because the reaction people have to a loss is more extreme than the reaction to the same gain. It's not just that people don't like to lose, it's how much more it distorts their thinking, anywhere from two to nine times as much. Please click on the options to learn a couple of the most important cognitive biases we run into. Please click on the options to learn a couple of the most important cognitive biases we run into.
